title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Know the Importance of Group Life Insurance This National Unity Day
National Unity Day is celebrated every year on 31st October. It is an Indian day that marks the birth anniversary of the Iron Man of India, Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. Sardar Patel was instrumental in uniting the segregated princely States of India together with the Union. It is because of his role that India is a united country comprising of different States and Union Territories. Thus, to honour the contributions and achievements of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the Government introduced 31st October as the National Unity Day.
The day symbolizes the unity that exists between the countrymen. This unity is also present in the members of a group and a group life insurance policy celebrates this unity.
What is a group life insurance plan?
A group life insurance policy is one that is issued to a group of individuals. The policy covers the members of a recognized or registered group. For example, employers can buy a group insurance plan for their employees, banks can buy the plan for their accountholders, trade unions can buy the coverage for their members, etc.
A Master policy is issued which covers all the members. The common form of group life insurance plans is term insurance plans that provide coverage against premature death. If any member dies during the coverage duration, the policy pays a lump sum benefit to the nominee or family of the deceased member. However, coverage for the other members continues uninterrupted.
Groups can buy a group life insurance policy for their members. They can pay the premium themselves or ask their members to contribute partially or completely.
What are the benefits of group insurance?
A group life insurance policy has multiple benefits which are why it is preferred by groups. The benefits of group insurance are as follows –
Easy availability
Group life insurance plans are easily available. Life insurers offer coverage and each insurer offers a comprehensive scope of coverage at competitive premiums. As such, groups can compare and choose from different types of policies available.
Easy coverage
The coverage under the policy is granted automatically if you are a member of a group that has bought the coverage. Usually, no individual health check-ups are needed and you can get covered even until very old age wherein life insurance plans disallow coverage.
Even for groups, availing of the coverage is an easy affair. They can apply with the insurance company, fill up the proposal form, pay the premium and get coverage for their members.
Affordable premiums
The group life insurance coverage is available at affordable premiums. As a member, if your group is paying for the plan, you can enjoy free coverage. On the other hand, if you pay the premium partially or fully, the outgo would not pinch your pockets. The premiums are quite affordable making it possible for you to pay for the coverage without putting a major dent in your budget.
A boost in morale
When a group invests in group life coverage for its members, it shows the members that the group actually cares for them. This boosts morale and fosters a sense of loyalty among members. They feel happy and proud to be a part of the group. This is especially relevant in the case of employer-employee groups wherein employers can retain and reward talented employees by offering them free group insurance coverage. Moreover, as the coverage takes care of the employees’ financial security, they can focus on their work. This also tends to boost employee productivity.
For these reasons, employers usually invest in group life insurance coverage as a part of their employee welfare programs.
Good coverage
Group life insurance plans allow good coverage to the members. The term life policies take care of the members’ family’s financial needs post their demise. Similarly, group superannuation and gratuity plans secure the employees’ incomes post-retirement or if they are eligible for gratuity payments.
Besides the inbuilt coverage benefits, group life insurance plans also allow optional riders to enhance the scope of coverage. Groups can enhance coverage by choosing suitable riders and providing better protection to their members.
Tax benefits
Lastly, group life insurance policies also have a tax benefit. If the group pays the premium for the coverage, it can claim the premium as a deductible expense from its taxable income. This brings down the taxable income and tax liability. Alternatively, if members pay for the coverage, they can claim tax benefits under Section 80C on the premium paid.
Conclusion
National Unity Day celebrates unity. Let the day celebrate the unity of a group too. Know the concept and benefits of a group life insurance plan. If you belong to a group, check if your group has opted for the coverage or not. If it did, know the coverage benefits. If not, motivate your group to opt for the coverage so that you can be financially secure.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Know the Importance of Group Life Insurance This National Unity Day
National Unity Day is celebrated every year on 31st October 1. It is an Indian day that marks the birth anniversary of the Iron Man of India, Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. Sardar Patel was instrumental in uniting the segregated princely States of India together with the Union. It is because of his role that India is a united country comprising of different States and Union Territories. Thus, to honour the contributions and achievements of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the Government introduced 31st October as the National Unity Day.
The day symbolizes the unity that exists between the countrymen. This unity is also present in the members of a group and a group life insurance policy celebrates this unity.
What is a group life insurance plan?
A group life insurance policy is one that is issued to a group of individuals. The policy covers the members of group. For example, employers can buy a group insurance plan for their employees, banks can buy the plan for their accountholders, trade unions can buy the coverage for their members, etc.
A Master policy is issued which covers all the members. The common form of group life insurance plans is term insurance plans that provide coverage against premature death. If any member dies during the coverage duration, the policy pays a lump sum benefit to the nominee or family of the deceased member. However, coverage for the other members continues uninterrupted.
Groups can buy a group life insurance policy for their members. They can pay the premium themselves or ask their members to contribute partially or completely.
What are the benefits of group insurance?
A group life insurance policy has multiple benefits which is why it is preferred by groups. Some benefits of group insurance are as follows –
1. Easy availability
Group life insurance plans are easily available. Life insurers offer a comprehensive scope of coverage depending on the requirements of the group at competitive premiums. As such, groups can compare and choose from different types of policies available. 2. Easy coverage
The coverage under the group policy is granted if you are a member of the group that has bought the coverage, subject to the terms and conditions of the group policy. Groups can apply with the insurance company, fill up the proposal form, pay the premium and get coverage for their members. 3. Affordable premiums
As premium is calculated considering the whole group, premium becomes affordable than the premium of an individual term-insurance policy. If the group master policyholder for the coverage of its members, e.g. Employer-Employee Group, pays the premium, individual members need not to pay for the insurance coverage. This affordable premium makes group plans beneficial for their members. 4. A boost in morale
When a group invests in group life coverage for its members, it shows the members that the group actually cares for them. This boosts morale and fosters a sense of loyalty among
members. This is especially relevant in the case of employer-employee groups wherein employers may retain and reward talented employees by offering them free group insurance coverage. Moreover, as the coverage takes care of the employees’ financial security, they can focus on their work. This also tends to boost employee productivity. For these reasons, employers usually invest in group life insurance coverage as a part of their employee welfare programs. 5. Life coverage
Group life insurance plans allow good coverage to the members. The term life policies take care of the members’ family’s financial needs post their demise. Similarly, group superannuation and gratuity plans secure the employees’ incomes post-retirement or if they are eligible for gratuity payments. Besides the inbuilt coverage benefits, group life insurance plans also allow optional riders on payment of nominal extra premium, to enhance the scope of coverage depending on the terms and conditions of the group policy. Groups can enhance coverage by choosing suitable riders and providing better protection to their members. 6. Tax benefits
Lastly, group life insurance policies also have a tax benefit. If the group pays the premium for the coverage, it can claim the premium as a deductible expense from its taxable income subject to provisions stated in the Income Tax Act, 1961. Alternatively, if members pay for the coverage, they can claim tax benefits under Section 80C of the abovementioned Act on the premium paid.
Conclusion
National Unity Day celebrates unity. Let the day celebrate the unity of a group too. Know the concept and benefits of a group life insurance plan. If you are a member of group, for e.g. You are a member of an employer-employee group or member of a non-employer-employee group, check if your group has opted for the coverage or not. If it did, know the coverage benefits. If not, you may motivate your group to opt for the coverage so that members can be financially secure.
Source:
